flashcards.a: A part of a line with two fixed endpoints.
A part of a line with one endpoint that extends indefinitely in one direction.
A straight path that extends indefinitely in both directions.
An angle is formed when two rays originate from the same endpoint called the vertex.

flashcards.q: What are the parts of an angle?
flashcards.a: The two rays (called arms) and the common endpoint (called the vertex).
An angle that measures between 0° and 90°.
An angle that measures exactly 90°.
An angle that measures greater than 90° but less than 180°.
An angle that measures exactly 180°.
An angle that measures greater than 180° but less than 360°.
Two angles whose measures add up to 90°.
Two angles whose measures add up to 180°.
Two angles that have a common vertex, a common arm, and their non-common arms on different sides of the common arm.

flashcards.q: What is a linear pair of angles?
flashcards.a: Two adjacent angles whose non-common arms form a straight line; they always sum to 180°.
If a ray stands on a line, then the sum of the two adjacent angles formed is 180°.

flashcards.q: What is the converse of the Linear Pair Axiom?
flashcards.a: If the sum of two adjacent angles is 180°, then the non-common arms form a line.
The pairs of non-adjacent angles formed when two lines intersect. They are always equal.
If two lines intersect, then the vertically opposite angles are equal.
Two lines that meet at a point called the point of intersection.
Two lines that do not intersect and maintain a constant distance from each other.

flashcards.q: What is the notation for parallel lines?
flashcards.a: l || m means line l is parallel to line m.
A line that intersects two or more lines at distinct points.
Three or more points that lie on the same line.
Points that do not all lie on the same line.
Angles that occupy the same relative position when a transversal intersects two lines. They are equal if the lines are parallel.
Angles on opposite sides of a transversal and between two lines. They are equal if the lines are parallel.
Angles on opposite sides of a transversal and outside two lines. They are equal if the lines are parallel.
Angles on the same side of a transversal and between two lines. They are supplementary (sum to 180°) if the lines are parallel.
Lines which are parallel to the same line are parallel to each other.
A ray that divides an angle into two equal angles.

flashcards.q: If an angle bisector divides a 78° angle, what is each resulting angle?
flashcards.a: Each resulting angle is 78° ÷ 2 = 39°.

flashcards.q: What is the sum of all angles around a point?
What does it mean for two lines to be perpendicular?
flashcards.a: They intersect at a right angle (90°). Denoted by ⊥.

flashcards.q: If one angle of a linear pair measures 65°, what is the other angle?
What is the relationship between the four angles formed by two intersecting lines?
flashcards.a: Vertically opposite angles are equal; adjacent angles are supplementary.

flashcards.q: Can two acute angles form a linear pair?
flashcards.a: No, because the sum of two acute angles is less than 180°.

flashcards.q: Can two obtuse angles form a linear pair?
flashcards.a: No, because the sum of two obtuse angles is greater than 180°.

flashcards.q: What must be true about one acute and one obtuse angle that form a linear pair?
flashcards.a: Their sum must equal 180°.

flashcards.q: When a transversal intersects two parallel lines, how many angles are equal to any given angle?
flashcards.a: Three angles are equal to any given angle (corresponding, alternate interior, or alternate exterior depending on position).

flashcards.q: What is the distance between two parallel lines?
flashcards.a: The length of a perpendicular drawn from one line to the other, which is constant at all points.

flashcards.q: If ray OR bisects ∠AOB and ray OS bisects ∠BOC, where ∠AOC = 180°, what is ∠ROS?
How many degrees is each angle if two angles in a linear pair are equal?
flashcards.a: Each angle is 90°.

flashcards.q: What is the difference between a linear pair and any two supplementary angles?
flashcards.a: A linear pair must be adjacent angles with non-common arms forming a line; supplementary angles can be any two angles summing to 180°.
